results.core_alignment_check: No substantive overlap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring processing security, while the contract only addresses post-termination continued processing/backups and deeming that processing in accordance with instructions.
results.condition_check: Regulation concerns assistance with security of processing; contract concerns processing for up to three months after termination and backup inclusion.
results.modality_check: Regulation imposes an assistance obligation; contract gives permission/authorization to continue processing and include data in backups, not security assistance.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and subject matter differ materially; the contract's post-termination processing and backup retention do not implement a security-assistance duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that any natural person acting under its authority with access to personal data processes them only on controller instructions. The contract says the processor's processing of the controller's personal data in the three months after termination is deemed to be in accordance with the Instruction, but it does not address natural persons acting under the processor's authority or impose the 'only on instructions' restriction on them.
results.condition_check: The regulation applies whenever a natural person under the processor's authority has access to personal data; the contract instead addresses a post-termination processing period and backup inclusion, which is a different situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory/deeming language for its own processing, but it does not impose the same obligation on the relevant actors covered by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing and scope are different: the contract is limited to a three-month post-termination period and backup-related processing, while the regulation is an ongoing access-and-instruction constraint for any natural person under the processor's authority.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Different obligation: the regulation requires processing personal data only on documented controller instructions, while the contract requires the processor to notify without undue delay if an instruction seems unlawful.
results.condition_check: Regulation is unconditional; contract is triggered only when an instruction is considered conflicting with applicable law.
results.modality_check: Contract imposes a notice duty, not the required processing-on-instructions duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No overlap on manner/timing/object beyond the separate notice obligation; the substantive processing instruction requirement is absent.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give notice without undue delay if the Data Processor considers the at the time being Instruction to be in conflict with the Applicable Law. 5. The data Processor’s obligations

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the processor to implement organizational and technical security measures under Art. 32(1); the contract obligates the Data Processor to implement appropriate technical and organizational measures in accordance with GDPR Article 32, which directly covers the same security obligation.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation describes measures that can include listed examples; the contract adopts the broader Article 32 obligation without narrowing the triggering situation.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Shall implement' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulation's requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract is broader and not looser on manner, timing, target, or location; it incorporates the Article 32 standard and does not contradict any of the listed security measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall implement the appropriate technical and organizational measures as set out in this Agreement and in the Applicable Law, including in accordance with GDPR, article 32.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to take into account security risks when assessing the level of security. The contract clause is about post-termination continued processing, backup inclusion, and deeming processing in accordance with instructions; it does not address security risk assessment or taking those risks into account.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching triggering condition for the security-assessment duty.
results.modality_check: Not applicable to the relevant duty; the clause does not impose the security-assessment obligation at all.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive overlap on manner, timing, target, location, or cause for the security-assessment requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, but the contract only requires maintaining a register of personal data breaches and does not impose any assistance/communication duty.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the regulation beyond the breach-communication context; the contract does not address that situation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall' for the register duty, but that duty is different from the required assistance duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant timing/manner/location constraints for the required assistance are addressed; the register clause is a different obligation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Partial overlap: both concern personal data breach notification/register content, and the contract sub-item states the nature of the breach plus some affected-data/subject counts. However, the regulation also requires the DPO name/contact details, consequences of the breach, and measures taken or proposed to mitigate effects, which are not present in the provided clause.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply to the personal data breach notification/register content, with the contract phrased as a register sub-item and the regulation as notification content.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is part of the required minimum contents.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible on timing/location insofar as the clause is an in-line required register item; but it omits several required content elements, so it is substantively incomplete.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the nature of the Personal Data Breach, including, if possible, the categories and the approximate number of affected Data Subjects and the categories and the approximate number of affected registrations of personal data.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to obtain prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engaging a sub-processor. The contract clause instead says the processor is accountable to the controller for sub-processors' actions and omissions; it addresses liability/accountability, not authorization or a precondition to engagement.
results.condition_check: The regulation has a prior written authorization condition; the contract clause has no comparable authorization condition.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a prohibition absent authorization; the contract imposes accountability, not permission/prohibition regarding engagement.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/location/manner equivalent to prior written authorization is provided.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both address processor processing of personal data when required by law despite the usual instruction-based framework; the contract says processing after termination is allowed/treated as in accordance with the Instruction when necessary and required under Applicable Law.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are conditioned on being required by Union/Member State or Applicable Law.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the regulation permits processing without documented instructions in that legal-required case, and the contract affirmatively allows/authorizes the processing in that case.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is not looser on the relevant constraint; it ties the processing to necessity and legal requirement, which matches the regulatory exception.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires that the breach notification include, among other items, the measures taken or proposed to mitigate the breach's effects. The contract sub-item, read with the mandatory parent clause introducing the register, states a description of the measures the processor has taken or proposes to take to address the personal data breach, including measures to mitigate its adverse effects. This is the same substantive information requirement, just framed as a register entry rather than a notification item.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both concern the personal data breach and the measures taken/proposed in response to it; the contract's 'where appropriate' qualifier is not weaker than the regulation's minimum content requirement.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The parent clause is mandatory ('shall have and maintain' / 'shall at a minimum include'), so the sub-item is part of a required register entry, not a mere permission.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The regulation asks for measures taken or proposed to mitigate effects; the contract covers measures taken or proposed to address the breach, including mitigation of adverse effects. No material mismatch in timing, target, or location.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the measures that the Data Processor has taken or proposes to take to address the Personal Data Breach, including, where appropriate, measures taken to mitigate its adverse effects.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text is only a heading/topic label ('Personal Data Breaches') and does not state any obligation to notify or include the required breach-notification contents.
results.condition_check: No condition stated in the contract text.
results.modality_check: No modality stated; the clause is not an operative obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, recipient, or content constraints are provided.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; the contract instead requires the processor's employees to process personal data only in accordance with the Instruction. This is a different, stricter confidentiality/authorization rule, not the same legal-exception obligation.
results.condition_check: The regulation's condition is 'if required by Union or Member State law'; the contract has no corresponding legal-exception condition and instead imposes an unconditional instruction-compliance requirement.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a permission/exception ('can process'); the contract is mandatory ('shall ensure' / 'only processes'). Although stricter wording can satisfy a shall, here it does not address the same permitted exception.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, or location constraint relevant to the legal-exception is provided; the contract's 'in accordance with the Instruction' is a different constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall also ensure that the Data Processor’s employees working processing the Personal Data only processes the Personal Data in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires processor assistance to the controller in prior consultation with supervisory authorities before high-risk processing; the contract requires immediate notice to the controller of a personal data breach.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: high-risk processing absent mitigation and prior consultation vs occurrence of a personal data breach.
results.modality_check: Both are mandatory, but they concern different duties.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and recipient differ; immediate breach notice is not equivalent to assisting prior consultation before processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both address processor liability/accountability for damage or misconduct arising in processing, including responsibility for others involved in processing. The regulation limits processor liability to non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to controller instructions; the contract makes the processor accountable to the controller for sub-processors as for its own acts/omissions, which is the same general liability allocation and is at least as strict.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation triggers liability where the processor has failed to comply with processor-specific GDPR duties or acted outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract states a broader accountability rule without narrowing those conditions, so it does not weaken the trigger.
results.modality_check: Compatible. 'Is accountable' imposes an obligation/liability at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall be liable'.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. 'For any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions' is at least as broad as the regulation's processor liability for processing-related damage and does not introduce a looser timing, manner, or scope constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is accountable to the Data Controller for any Sub-Processor in the same way as for its own actions and omissions.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure that natural persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on the controller's instructions. The contract instead requires written sub-processor agreements, equivalent data protection obligations for sub-processors, and ongoing monitoring/control of sub-processor compliance. This addresses sub-processors and compliance generally, not the specific instruction-only processing duty for persons acting under the processor's authority.
results.condition_check: No special triggering condition beyond persons acting under the processor's authority having access to personal data; the contract targets sub-processors and compliance monitoring instead.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory obligations, but they are obligations of a different kind than the regulation's instruction-only processing requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's target is any natural person acting under the processor's authority with access to personal data; the contract's target is sub-processors and their agreements/monitoring, which is a different locus and does not directly impose the same constraint on persons processing data only on controller instructions.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall conclude a written sub-processor agreement with any Sub-Processors. Such an agreement shall at minimum provide the same data protection obligations as the ones applicable to the Data Processor, including the obligations under this Data Processor Agreement. The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, while the contract only requires forwarding data-subject rights requests to the controller and not responding directly; it does not address breach communication assistance.
results.condition_check: Different trigger: the contract applies when a data subject makes a rights request, not when there is a personal data breach communication to be made.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory, but on a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant overlap on timing or manner for breach communication assistance.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the breach notification to include the consequences of the breach, and the contract sub-item states a description of the likely and actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.
results.condition_check: No special condition in either text; both apply to the personal data breach notification/register content.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the parent clause is mandatory ('shall') and the sub-item is mandatory content within that register.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract is at least as specific on the consequences element, covering both likely and actually occurred consequences, which meets the required consequences information.
results.contract_source_text: A description of the likely as well as actually occurred consequences of the Personal Data Breach.

shared_groups: Controller Rights Over Processing Changes
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation gives the controller a right to terminate the DPA in certain cases; the contract instead says the processor’s authorization to process personal data ends when the DPA terminates. This addresses termination consequences, not the controller’s right to terminate.
results.condition_check: The regulation is conditional ('in certain cases'); the contract has no matching conditional termination-right framework.
results.modality_check: The regulation requires a right for the controller; the contract imposes an automatic effect on processor authorization, not the controller’s right.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and locus: termination of authorization upon agreement termination is not the same as the controller’s right to terminate the DPA.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s authorization to process Personal Data on behalf of the Data Controller shall be annulled at the termination of this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified content (nature of breach, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract only requires providing a copy of the register of personal data breaches on written request; that is an information-access duty about a register, not the content of the Article 33(3) notification.
results.condition_check: The contract is triggered only if the Data Controller or relevant Data Protection Agency requests it in writing; the regulation requires inclusion in the breach notification whenever notification is made.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall be provided' obligation, but it is about a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and recipient: a copy of the register, not the notification content; on request in writing, which is not the same as the breach-notification content requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests; the contract instead requires the processor to notify the controller immediately of personal data breaches. Different duty, different object, different trigger.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different condition: rights requests versus data security breach.
results.modality_check: Not applicable to the required obligation; the contract uses a mandatory notice duty, but it is for a different subject.
results.other_constraints_check: Timing and target differ, but the main issue is lack of substantive overlap with the requested assistance obligation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall give immediate notice to the Data Controller if a breach of the data security occurs, that can lead to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of or access to, personal data transmitted, stored or otherwise processed re the Personal Data processed on behalf of the Data Controller (a “Personal Data Breach”).

The contract clause instead grants general authorization to use sub-processors and imposes notice/objection mechanics; it does not state processor liability for sub-processor obligations.

results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent coverage of the 'remain fully liable' obligation; the clause addresses authorization and objection timing, not liability scope.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation permits processing without documented instructions when required by Union or Member State law; the contract instead imposes confidentiality and a general prohibition on processing contrary to instructions unless the controller agrees in writing.

results.modality_check: The contract is more restrictive, but it does not address the same permission/exception structure.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching substantive overlap on the specific lawful-processing exception; confidentiality is adjacent but not the same obligation.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

results.modality_check: Both are mandatory, but modality does not cure the mismatch.
results.other_constraints_check: Different target and subject matter; no substantive overlap with assistance in handling rights requests.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller; obligation is to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors under general written authorization, and the clause requires written notice of the identity of a potential sub-processor before engagement/processing, which covers intended addition/replacement changes
results.condition_check: Compatible: both are triggered in the context of general written authorization for sub-processors
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: the contract imposes a mandatory written notification obligation, not mere permission

results.core_alignment_check: Yes: the regulation requires the DPA to state the duration of processing, and the contract specifies a concrete processing duration/retention period of up to three months after termination.

results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language ('shall'), but on a different obligation.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract states categories/types of personal data processed, not the categories of data subjects (classes of persons) required by the regulation.

results.modality_check: Compatible: 'will process' is an affirmative commitment, but it is to process data, not to state data-subject categories.

results.contract_source_text: In connection with the Data Processor’s delivery of the Main Services to the Data Controller, the Data Processor will process certain categories and types of the Data Controller’s personal data on behalf of the Data Controller.

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in ensuring processing security, while the contract only addresses forwarding data-subject rights requests and not security assistance.

results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: Same actor (processor) and same subject matter (personal data after service termination), but the contract only permits/continues processing for a limited post-termination period and does not require return or deletion to the controller.
results.condition_check: Regulation triggers after the end of provision of services relating to processing; contract triggers after termination of the agreement and allows processing for up to three months if necessary/required by law. This is not the same obligation and is materially different.
results.modality_check: Regulation imposes a mandatory return-or-delete duty; contract uses permission/entitlement to continue processing and backup inclusion, which is not an equal-or-greater obligation to return/delete.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds a post-termination retention/processing window and backup inclusion, which is contrary to the required return-or-delete outcome and does not satisfy it.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the DPA to contain the processor's identity and contact details, while the contract clause requires the processor to maintain a register of processing activities; these are different obligations and different content.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition in either text.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it is directed to a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, target, or location constraints relevant to the required identity/contact details content.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of processing activities in accordance with GDPR, article 32 (2).

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the categories of data subjects (classes of persons), while the contract states the types of personal data (kinds of data); wrong axis.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations to the controller; the contract only requires maintaining a register of processing activities, which is an internal record and does not itself provide or make available the required information to the controller.
results.condition_check: Compatible in the sense that both concern compliance-related records, but the contract does not impose the same triggering duty to provide information on request or for inspection.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it is directed to maintaining a register rather than making information available.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent target/recipient or disclosure-to-controller requirement is present; the clause is about recordkeeping, not availability or assistance.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of processing activities in accordance with GDPR, article 32 (2).

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes: both concern the processor's use/engagement of sub-processors and the contract addresses adding new sub-processors to a listed set, which is the same real-world obligation area.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the regulation requires prior specific or general written authorization before engaging a sub-processor; the contract requires any new sub-processor to be added to the list, which is consistent with a controlled/authorized sub-processor regime, though it does not expressly state the authorization condition.
results.modality_check: Weaker/insufficient: the contract imposes a listing/update requirement but does not expressly require prior specific or general written authorization from the controller before engagement.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing/constraint is not equivalent: the regulation requires prior authorization before engagement, while the contract only requires post-initiation addition to a list in an appendix.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. If the Data Processor initiates sub-processing with a new Sub-Processor, such new Sub-Processor shall be added to the list in sub-appendix B under paragraph 2. 7. Remuneration and costs

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the DPIA itself to include specified substantive elements (description of processing and purposes, necessity/proportionality assessment, risk assessment, and measures to address risks). The contract instead obligates the processor to assist the controller in preparing DPIAs and prior consultation; it does not itself state the DPIA content, but it does address the same DPIA-related obligation as processor assistance.
results.condition_check: Compatible: the processor must assist only if its assistance is necessary and relevant, which is a reasonable qualifier and not weaker than the GDPR assistance duty.
results.modality_check: Equal or stronger: 'shall assist' is mandatory.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the assistance is tied to preparing DPIAs under GDPR Article 35 and prior consultation under Article 36, which matches the regulatory context.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance with Article 28 obligations; the contract only requires providing a copy of the personal data breaches register on written request, which is a narrower, specific record and not the full compliance-information duty.
results.condition_check: Not compatible: the regulation is an ongoing availability duty, while the contract triggers only upon a written request by the controller or data protection agency.
results.modality_check: Weaker/narrower: 'shall be provided ... if so requested' is conditional and limited to a specific register, not the broader mandatory availability of compliance information.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and scope: a breaches register is not the same as information necessary to demonstrate compliance with all Article 28 obligations; the recipient/request mechanism is also narrower than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: The register of Personal Data Breaches shall be provided to the Data Controller in copy if so requested in writing by the Data Controller or the relevant Data Protection Agency.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text is only a heading/topic label ('Personal Data Breaches') and does not state any notification content or obligation matching the required breach notification particulars.
results.condition_check: No condition stated.
results.modality_check: No modality stated.
results.other_constraints_check: No manner/timing/target/location/cause constraints stated.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires a specific processor duty to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process only on controller instructions; the contract clause is only a general commitment to comply with applicable data protection/privacy law and does not itself state that specific instruction-only restriction.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition is stated in the contract clause beyond general compliance with law; it does not match the regulation's access-to-personal-data condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall ensure' formulation, but it is directed to a different, broader obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No equivalent timing, manner, target, or location constraints for the specific instruction-only processing duty are provided.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor Agreement shall ensure that the Data Processor complies with the applicable data protection and privacy legislation (the ”Applicable Law”), including in particular:

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller before processing when a legal requirement applies, while the contract sub-item only says the processor complies with applicable law in processing the controller’s personal data; it does not impose any duty to inform or notify the controller.
results.condition_check: The regulation is conditional on a legal requirement and a public-interest non-prohibition; the contract clause has no comparable notification condition because it does not address notification at all.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory informing obligation; the contract clause is a general compliance statement, not an obligation to inform the controller.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing requirement ('before processing') and the specific recipient ('the controller') are absent from the contract clause; the parent context about providing documentation on written request concerns substantiation, not advance notice of legal requirements.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for performance of sub-processor obligations; the contract instead imposes a general cap on each party’s cumulative liability for breach of contract.
results.condition_check: The regulation is unconditional; the contract’s cap applies generally upon breach and does not preserve full liability for sub-processor performance.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory limitation language, but it is a liability cap rather than an obligation to remain fully liable.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s timing/lookback period and proportional calculation are unrelated to the regulation’s required scope of liability.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's obligations, i.e. an uncapped/continuing liability allocation for sub-processor performance. The contract instead limits each party’s cumulative liability to a capped amount based on prior payments, which is a different and materially narrower liability regime and does not address sub-processor obligations.
results.condition_check: No relevant matching condition; the contract speaks generally about liability caps upon breach, not liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory liability obligation; the contract imposes a liability cap, which is contradictory/looser rather than equal-or-greater force.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation’s target is liability to the controller for sub-processor performance; the contract’s target is a general cap on each party’s liability under the agreement, with no equivalent sub-processor-performance allocation.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the breach notification itself to include specified information (nature, DPO contact details, consequences, mitigation measures). The contract only requires the processor to maintain a register of breaches and says the register shall include following items, but it does not itself state those items here or require the notification content.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition is stated in either text beyond the existence of a personal data breach; the contract does not add a conflicting condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory 'shall' for maintaining the register and including items, which is not weaker than the regulation's 'shall at least include' wording, but it is about a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation concerns the content of a breach notification; the contract concerns keeping a register. Different object and locus, so the timing/target/location constraints do not align as the same duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall have and maintain a register of all Personal Data Breaches. The register shall at a minimum include the following:

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation is about the processor's liability for damage caused by processing, limited to two situations: non-compliance with GDPR processor-specific obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract clause instead addresses remuneration for adapting processing to changed instructions and an exemption from liability for non-performance of the Main Agreement when performance conflicts with changed instructions or delivery is impossible. It does not state the processor's liability for processing damage or the GDPR liability conditions.
results.condition_check: The contract's condition is conflict with changed instructions or impossibility of delivery, which is not the same as liability only where the processor failed GDPR processor obligations or acted outside/contrary to lawful instructions.
results.modality_check: The contract uses an exemption from liability for non-performance and a right to remuneration, not the regulation's liability rule for damage caused by processing.
results.other_constraints_check: The subject matter overlaps only loosely around instructions and liability, but the target duty, object, and legal consequence are different; the clause concerns contractual performance and pricing, not Article 82 processor damage liability.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is also entitled to remuneration for any time and material used to adapt and change the processing activities in order to comply with any changes to the Data Controller’s Instruction, including implementation costs and additional costs required to deliver the Main Services due to the change in the Instruction. The Data Processor is exempted from liability for non-performance with the Main Agreement if the performance of the obligations under the Main Agreement would be in conflict with any changed Instruction or if contractual delivery in accordance with the changed Instruction is impossible. This could for instance be the case;

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to bind engaged sub-processors by contract or other legal act to the same Article 28(3) obligations. The contract clause only gives the processor general authorization to engage sub-processors and requires prior notice/objection mechanics; it does not impose the Article 28(3) obligations on sub-processors.
results.condition_check: No relevant matching condition; the clause concerns authorization and notice before engaging sub-processors, not the required contractual flow-down of obligations.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory duty on the processor. The contract clause is permissive/authorizing with notice requirements, not a mandatory obligation to impose the GDPR Article 28(3) terms on sub-processors.
results.other_constraints_check: The required manner is by contract or other legal act under Union or Member State law; the clause does not provide this flow-down mechanism or equivalent stricter constraint.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Personal Data Breach Notification And Response
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text only gives the heading 'Personal Data Breaches' and does not state any notification content, actor, recipient, or required items.
results.condition_check: No substantive condition is stated.
results.modality_check: No obligation modality is stated.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, or content constraints are stated.
results.contract_source_text: Personal Data Breaches

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires processor assistance to the controller with DPIA compliance, while the contract only requires providing documentation of security measures on written request; that is an audit/documentation duty, not DPIA assistance.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is triggered by the need to assist with DPIA compliance, whereas the contract is triggered only by a written request for documentation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', which is mandatory, but it mandates a different duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract is narrower and different in object and purpose (security-measures documentation vs DPIA assistance); no equivalent timing/location/target overlap.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the DPA to state the types of personal data processed, while the contract text only names a different topic ('Rights of the data subjects') and does not identify any personal data types.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition stated in either text.
results.modality_check: No operative modality in the contract text; it is just a heading/topic label.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing, manner, location, or other constraints are provided.
results.contract_source_text: Rights of the data subjects

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation sets a processor-liability rule for damage caused by processing, limited to two specific fault situations (non-compliance with GDPR processor obligations or acting outside/contrary to controller instructions). The contract instead sets a general contractual cap on each party’s cumulated liability for breach of contract based on prior payments. Same broad subject of liability, but not the same obligation.
results.condition_check: Not applicable in substance: the contract’s trigger is occurrence of circumstances leading to a breach of contract, not the regulation’s processor-specific fault conditions.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a liability rule; the contract is also mandatory, but it is a different liability regime and does not track the required processor-liability standard.
results.other_constraints_check: The scope, target, and basis differ materially: GDPR damage caused by processing versus contractual breach liability capped by payments and time period. This is not a stricter or equivalent formulation of the same duty.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation concerns when a processor is liable for damage caused by processing: liability only if it failed GDPR processor-specific obligations or acted outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions. The contract clause instead sets a general cap on each party’s cumulated liability under the agreement based on prior payments; it does not address the processor’s liability triggers, exceptions, or the lawful-instructions/GDPR-breach conditions.
results.condition_check: No matching condition. The regulation is conditional on non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful instructions; the contract conditions liability on occurrence of circumstances leading to a breach of contract and the amount of prior payments.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a substantive liability rule; the contract imposes a monetary limitation on liability. This is not the same modality or obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract’s timing and calculation constraints (12 months of payments, proportional calculation if shorter) are unrelated to the regulation’s scope and exceptions for processor liability.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires a processor to demonstrate guarantees for Article 28(1)-(4) via adherence to an approved code of conduct or certification mechanism; the contract only requires documentation of compliance to be provided within a reasonable time, which is an information-production/timing duty and does not mention guarantees, codes of conduct, certification, or the Article 28(1)-(4) demonstration mechanism.
results.condition_check: No matching condition; the regulation is about demonstrating guarantees through specific compliance mechanisms, while the contract is about providing documentation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall', but it is mandatory for a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: 'Within reasonable time' is not the relevant substantive constraint for the regulation's mechanism-based demonstration requirement.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the cited sub-item is only a reference to applicable legislation (Directive 95/46/EF and Danish implementing law) and, together with the parent, merely says the processor must comply with applicable law. It does not itself state the specific obligation to process personal data only on documented instructions from the controller.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition is stated for the Art. 28(3a) duty in the contract text.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, but the sub-item content is only a legal citation/pointer, not an operative obligation matching the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: No substantive timing, manner, target, or location constraints corresponding to 'documented instructions from the controller' are provided.
results.contract_source_text: The European Parliament and the Council’s Directive 95/46/EF of 24 October 1995 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data as implemented in Danish law with, among others, the Act on Processing of Personal Data (Act No. 429 of 31 May 2000).

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in responding to data subject rights requests. The contract requires the processor, when it receives such a request, to immediately forward it to the controller and not respond directly. This is related assistance, but it does not itself provide broader assistance in fulfilling the controller's obligation to respond; it only forwards the request and avoids direct response.
results.condition_check: Both are triggered by a data subject request to exercise data subject rights, so the condition is substantially the same.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'must', which is at least as strong as the regulation's 'shall'.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds immediacy and a prohibition on direct response, which are not weaker than the regulation.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the DPA to state the types of personal data processed, i.e. a content/disclosure obligation about data categories. The contract clause instead restricts processing to documented instructions and a service purpose; it does not itself name or set out any types of personal data.
results.condition_check: No relevant condition overlap; the regulation is an inclusion/content requirement, while the contract is an instruction-and-purpose restriction.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a restrictive 'may only' modality, but that does not cure the absence of the required content.
results.other_constraints_check: No matching timing, manner, or location constraint; the clause points to the Main Agreement for service purpose, not to types of personal data.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: Data Return Deletion And Post-Termination Handling
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to return or delete all personal data to the controller after the end of processing services. The contract instead allows continued processing for up to three months after termination, with backup inclusion and deemed-instruction compliance; it does not require return or deletion of all personal data to the controller.
results.condition_check: Both are triggered after the end/termination of the processing relationship, but the contract adds a post-termination processing period rather than the required return/delete action.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory return-or-delete duty. The contract uses permissive/allowing language for continued processing and backup inclusion, not an equal-or-greater obligation to return or delete.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing is not equivalent: the regulation requires action after services end, while the contract permits processing for up to three months after termination. The target/recipient also differs because the required recipient is the controller for return, which is absent.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processor performance; the contract instead requires the controller to indemnify the processor for additional costs from legal changes.
results.condition_check: Different condition: regulation is unconditional liability allocation for sub-processor obligations; contract is triggered by changes in applicable law causing additional costs.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory indemnity, but it is a different obligation and direction.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter, recipient, and target: indemnification for costs versus liability for sub-processor obligations.
results.contract_source_text: If changes to the Applicable Law, including new guidance or courts practice, result in additional costs to the Data Processor, the Data Controller shall indemnify the Data Processor of such documented costs. 8. Breach and liability

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to assess security by taking into account specified risks to personal data; the contract only requires providing documentation of security measures on written request, which is an information/disclosure duty, not the risk-assessment obligation itself.
results.condition_check: Compatible only in the sense that both relate to security, but the contract is triggered by a written request rather than by assessing security level; the triggering situation is different.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it imposes a different duty, not the required assessment duty.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation’s object is the processor’s security assessment considering risks to personal data; the contract’s object is documentation of security measures provided to the controller on request. Different target and substance.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for sub-processors' performance. The contract only identifies/list sub-processors and requires adding new ones to a list; it does not impose liability or responsibility for their obligations.
results.condition_check: Not applicable; the contract does not address the liability condition at all.
results.modality_check: Not applicable; no liability obligation is stated.
results.other_constraints_check: Not applicable; the clause concerns listing sub-processors, not liability for their obligations.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement using the Sub- Processors listed in sub-appendix B. shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires DPIA content/assessments under Article 35(7), while the contract obligates the processor to assist the controller in preparing DPIAs in accordance with Article 35. This is the same real-world obligation at the assistance level, and the processor-side assistance duty is the correct processor analogue.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract limits the duty to when the processor's assistance is necessary and relevant, which is a reasonable and expected qualifier and does not weaken the obligation beyond the regulation's scope.
results.modality_check: Compliant. 'Shall assist' is mandatory and at least as strong as the regulatory requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract specifically ties the assistance to preparing DPIAs under GDPR Article 35 and also includes prior consultation under Article 36, which is consistent with and broader than the regulation's DPIA requirement.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor’s assistance is necessary and relevant, the Data Processor shall assist the Data Controller in preparing data protection impact assessments in accordance with GDPR, article 35, along with any prior consultation in accordance with GDPR, article 36.

shared_groups: Controller Rights Over Processing Changes
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation gives the controller a right to suspend processing in certain cases, while the contract concerns processor authorization to engage sub-processors and the controller's right to object to them; different actor, object, and remedy.
results.condition_check: Not aligned: the regulation is about certain cases for suspension of processing, whereas the contract conditions concern notice and objection to sub-processors.
results.modality_check: Not aligned: the regulation grants a right to the controller; the contract grants authorization to the processor and sets an objection procedure.
results.other_constraints_check: Not aligned: the contract's timing/notice/7-day objection mechanics relate to sub-processor engagement, not suspension of processing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor is given general authorization to engage third-parties to process the Personal Data (“Sub-Processors”) without obtaining any further written, specific authorization from the Data Controller, provided that the Data Processor notifies the Data Controller in writing about the identity of a potential Sub-Processor (and its processors, if any) before any agreements are made with the relevant Sub-Processors and before the relevant Sub-Processor processes any of the Personal Data. If the Data Controller wish to object to the relevant Sub- Processor, the Data Controller shall give notice hereof in writing within seven

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Processor security assessment must consider risks to personal data; contract imposes confidentiality and processing restrictions on personal data, which is related but not the same obligation and does not expressly require risk assessment or taking those risks into account.
results.condition_check: Regulation applies when assessing the level of security; contract applies generally to handling personal data and confidentiality, with no equivalent security-assessment trigger.
results.modality_check: Regulation requires the processor to take into account specified risks; contract uses mandatory confidentiality/processing prohibitions, but not the required assessment duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Regulation targets accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure, or access in relation to transmitted, stored, or processed data; contract only addresses confidentiality and processing contrary to instructions, without covering the assessment of those security risks.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text is only a heading/topic label ('Data protection impact assessments and prior consultation') and does not itself state any DPIA content requirements such as the processing description, necessity/proportionality assessment, risk assessment, or mitigation measures.
results.condition_check: No condition stated.
results.modality_check: No operative modality stated; only a heading.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/location/content constraints stated.

shared_groups: Controller Rights Over Processing Changes
Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both address the controller's right to object to the processor's intended addition/replacement of sub-processors after prior written notice. The contract gives the controller a right to object within a notice period after notification, which matches the regulation's objection right under general written authorization.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The regulation requires the objection right after the controller has been informed of intended changes; the contract requires written notice of the potential sub-processor before engagement and allows objection within seven calendar days of receiving that notification.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The regulation says the controller shall have the right to object; the contract likewise grants a right to object and makes absence of objection operate as consent, which is not weaker than the required right.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The timing and target are aligned: notice is given before the sub-processor is engaged and before processing begins, and the objection must be made within seven calendar days of notification, which is a reasonable and specific implementation of the post-notification objection window.
results.contract_source_text: calendar days from receiving the notification from the Data Processor. Absence of any objections from the Data Controller shall be deemed a consent to the relevant Sub-Processor.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. Both provisions concern processor security/access controls for personal data. The regulation requires the processor, when assessing security, to take into account risks of unauthorized access/disclosure and related harms to personal data; the contract requires restricting access to personal data to employees with a need to process it, which is a substantive security measure addressing unauthorized access.
results.condition_check: Compatible. The contract is an affirmative security/access restriction without a conflicting trigger; it applies generally to access to personal data, which is at least as protective as taking account of the specified risks when assessing security.
results.modality_check: Compatible. The contract uses 'shall ensure,' which is mandatory and not weaker than the regulation's 'shall take into account.'
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract's limitation to employees who need access is a stricter target constraint than the regulation's broader risk-based security assessment requirement; it does not contradict the timing, manner, or scope of the regulatory duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall ensure that access to the Personal Data is restricted to only the employees to whom it is necessary and relevant to process the Personal Data in order for the Data Processor to perform its obligations under the Main Agreement and this Data Processor Agreement.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes processor liability for damage caused by processing only in specified breach scenarios (non-compliance with processor-directed GDPR obligations or acting outside/contrary to lawful controller instructions). The contract clause instead requires the controller to indemnify the processor for documented additional costs caused by changes in applicable law/guidance/court practice. Different actor, different object, different trigger, and different kind of liability/indemnity.
results.condition_check: No overlap: the regulation is triggered by damage caused by processing and processor misconduct; the contract is triggered by changes in law/guidance/court practice causing additional costs.
results.modality_check: The regulation is a liability rule; the contract is an affirmative indemnity obligation. Not the same obligation and not a stricter version of the same duty.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter and remedy: damage liability under GDPR versus cost indemnification for legal-change impacts. No matching timing, target, or causal constraints.
results.contract_source_text: If changes to the Applicable Law, including new guidance or courts practice, result in additional costs to the Data Processor, the Data Controller shall indemnify the Data Processor of such documented costs. 8. Breach and liability

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller with DPIA compliance; the contract instead imposes a breach-notification duty for personal data breaches.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / different trigger: DPIA assistance versus occurrence of a personal data breach.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures

shared_groups: Assistance To Controller
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract is about providing documentation of the processor’s security measures on request, not assisting the controller in notifying a personal data breach to the supervisory authority.

results.other_constraints_check: Different object and recipient; no breach-notification assistance or supervisory authority notification timing/content.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to allow for and contribute to audits/inspections by the controller or its auditor; the contract only requires documentation of compliance to be provided within reasonable time, which is an information-sharing duty, not audit/inspection assistance.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the contract matches the audit/inspection context of the regulation.
results.modality_check: The contract uses 'shall', but it mandates provision of documentation rather than audit participation.
results.other_constraints_check: The timing 'within reasonable time' is not the same as allowing/contributing to audits or inspections, and the target/object differs materially.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor’s documentation of compliance shall be provided within reasonable time.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to immediately inform the controller when an instruction infringes GDPR or other data protection law. The contract clause instead addresses post-termination processing, backup inclusion, and deeming processing to be in accordance with the instruction; it does not impose any duty to notify the controller of unlawful instructions.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching triggering condition. The regulation is triggered by an instruction that infringes GDPR or other data protection provisions; the contract clause is triggered by termination and a three-month post-termination period.
results.modality_check: The regulation imposes a mandatory notification duty. The contract clause contains permissions and deeming language about processing, not a notification obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation requires immediacy and notification to the controller. The contract clause has a different subject matter, different timing, and no comparable notice target or timing.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to remain fully liable to the controller for a sub-processor's obligations. The contract clause instead says the main agreement's breach-of-contract rules and consequences apply to this DPA as an integrated part of the main agreement; it does not allocate liability for sub-processors' obligations to the processor or to the controller.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition in the regulation beyond the processor's liability for sub-processor obligations; the contract clause concerns general breach consequences, not that condition.
results.modality_check: The contract uses mandatory language for applying breach rules, but it is about a different obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and target: breach-of-contract consequences under the main agreement versus liability for sub-processor obligations to the controller. No substantive overlap on the required liability duty.
results.contract_source_text: The Main Agreement’s regulation of breach of contract and the consequences hereof shall apply equally to this Data Processor Agreement as if this Data Processor Agreement is an integrated part hereof.

results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject. results.other_constraints_check: The timing constraint ('immediately') is stricter, but it applies to forwarding requests, not breach communication.
results.contract_source_text: If the Data Processor receives a request from a data subject for the exercise of the data subject’s rights under the Applicable Law and such request is related to the Personal Data of the Data Controller, the Data Processor must immediately forward the request to the Data Controller and must refrain from responding to the person directly.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight

results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation requires the processor to assist the controller in communicating a personal data breach to the data subject, while the contract sub-item only specifies content for a breach register (a description of the breach, affected data subjects, and registrations). That is record content, not assistance in communicating to data subjects.
