shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope
results.core_alignment_check: Yes. The regulation requires the DPA to state the nature and purpose of the processing. The contract states the processor may only process personal data for the purpose of delivering the Main Services, which covers the purpose of processing; the nature of processing is also indicated by acting/processing only in accordance with documented instructions.
results.condition_check: Compatible. Both are about the processing under the DPA; the contract adds the condition that processing must follow the controller's documented instruction.
results.modality_check: Compatible and stricter. The contract uses 'may only' and 'may only process', which is restrictive and at least as strong as a requirement to contain the information.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible. The contract specifies the purpose of processing in-line, not by cross-reference alone, and does not contradict the required content.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor may only act and process the Personal Data in accordance with the documented instruction from the Data Controller (the ”Instruction”). The Instruction at the time of entering into this Data Processor Agreement is that the Data Processor may only process the Personal Data with the purpose of delivering the Main Services as described in the Main Agreement.

shared_groups: DPA Party Identification And Processing Scope

shared_groups: Compliance Demonstration And Audit Cooperation
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the regulation requires the processor to demonstrate Article 28(1)-(4) guarantees via adherence to an approved code of conduct or approved certification mechanism; the contract sub-item only states general compliance with Applicable Law for processing, which is a different and broader duty and does not mention codes of conduct, certification, or demonstrating those guarantees.
results.condition_check: No specific triggering condition overlap beyond general compliance; the regulation is conditional on demonstrating guarantees through approved codes/certification, which is absent.
results.modality_check: The parent clause imposes a mandatory documentation duty on request, but the sub-item itself is only a substantive compliance statement and does not impose the specific demonstration mechanism required by the regulation.
results.other_constraints_check: The regulation's specific means (approved codes of conduct or certification mechanism) are missing; general lawful processing compliance is not the same target or method.
results.contract_source_text: the Data Processor complies with the Applicable Law in respect of the processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data.

shared_groups: Data Protection Impact Assessment
results.core_alignment_check: The contract text is only a heading/topic label ('Data protection impact assessments and prior consultation') and does not itself state any obligation to include the required DPIA contents (processing description, necessity/proportionality assessment, risk assessment, or measures).
results.condition_check: No condition stated.
results.modality_check: No operative modality stated; only a heading.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/manner/target/location details stated; no substantive content overlap.
results.contract_source_text: Data protection impact assessments and prior consultation

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: Gap: the contract text is only an unfilled placeholder ('[insert when relevant]') and does not itself state any obligation about controller objection rights, notice of intended sub-processor changes, or changes to add/replace sub-processors. The parent context merely says sub-processors have been added and communicated prior to sub-processing, which is not the controller's right to object after being informed.
results.condition_check: No substantive condition is stated in the contract clause; the parent context concerns prior communication of added sub-processors, but not the specific trigger of intended changes or the controller's objection right.
results.modality_check: No operative modality is present in the placeholder clause. The parent context is descriptive, not an obligation granting a right to object.
results.other_constraints_check: No timing/target/location constraints matching the regulation's 'after having been informed of intended changes' and 'changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors' are provided in the operative clause.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation imposes GDPR damage liability on any controller involved in processing for damage caused by infringing processing. The contract clause instead caps each party’s contractual liability under the DPA to a payment-based amount, which is a different subject and does not allocate liability for GDPR processing damage to controllers.
results.condition_check: Not applicable: the contract clause is a general liability cap tied to breach of contract timing, not the GDPR infringement/damage condition in the regulation.
results.modality_check: Weaker/different: the regulation creates liability; the contract limits liability. This is not an equal-or-greater obligation to be liable for GDPR damage.
results.other_constraints_check: Different object and scope: contractual liability under the Data Processor Agreement versus damage caused by processing infringing the GDPR. The timing/payment calculation details do not address the regulatory duty.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage
results.core_alignment_check: No: the regulation imposes liability on any controller involved in processing for damage caused by GDPR-infringing processing, while the contract limits each party’s cumulative contractual liability to a payment-based cap for breach of contract; this is a different obligation and does not address GDPR damage liability.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching triggering condition; the contract speaks to breach of contract and payment history, not damage caused by processing that infringes the GDPR.
results.modality_check: The contract is mandatory as a liability cap, but it is not the same kind of obligation as the regulation’s liability rule.
results.other_constraints_check: The target and cause differ materially: contractual liability for breach of contract versus statutory liability for damage caused by infringing processing.
results.contract_source_text: Each party’s cumulated liability under this Data Processor Agreement is limited to the payments made under the Main Agreement in the 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract. If the Data Processor Agreement has not been in force for 12 months before the occurrence of the circumstances leading to a breach of contract, the limited liability amount shall be calculated proportionately based on the actual performed payments.

shared_groups: Processing Instructions And Legal Basis Compliance
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires a processor obligation to ensure persons acting under its authority with access to personal data process them only on controller instructions. The contract sub-item merely cites a directive and Danish law as part of the definition of Applicable Law; it does not itself impose the instruction-only processing rule.
results.condition_check: Not applicable / no matching condition stated.
results.modality_check: The parent clause is mandatory, but the sub-item is only a legal citation, not an operative obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: No relevant manner/timing/target/location constraints are stated in the sub-item.
results.contract_source_text: The European Parliament and the Council’s Directive 95/46/EF of 24 October 1995 on the protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data as implemented in Danish law with, among others, the Act on Processing of Personal Data (Act No. 429 of 31 May 2000).

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to take measures required under Article 32 to ensure processing security. The contract clause instead addresses post-termination continued processing for up to three months, backup inclusion, and deeming that processing to be in accordance with instructions; it does not impose security measures or Article 32 safeguards.
results.condition_check: No matching security/Article 32 condition; the contract is limited to a post-termination period and legal necessity for continued processing.
results.modality_check: The contract uses permission/authorization for backup inclusion and a deeming statement, not an obligation to implement security measures.
results.other_constraints_check: Different subject matter and target: continued processing after termination versus security of processing measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.

shared_groups: Sub-Processor Authorization And Oversight
results.core_alignment_check: processor/controller: the regulation requires the processor to inform the controller of intended changes concerning addition or replacement of sub-processors; the contract parent clause requires the processor to notify the controller in writing about the identity of a potential sub-processor before agreements are made and before processing, with an objection window for the controller. This covers notice of intended sub-processor changes/additions/replacements.
results.condition_check: Compatible: both apply in the context of general written authorization for sub-processors.
results.modality_check: Compatible: the contract imposes a mandatory notification duty, which meets the regulatory 'shall inform' requirement.
results.other_constraints_check: Compatible: the contract requires notice before agreements are made and before processing, which is at least as strict as informing of intended changes.
results.contract_source_text: calendar days from receiving the notification from the Data Processor. Absence of any objections from the Data Controller shall be deemed a consent to the relevant Sub-Processor.

shared_groups: Security Of Processing Measures
results.core_alignment_check: No. The regulation requires implementing/maintaining security measures (including pseudonymisation, encryption, confidentiality, integrity, availability/resilience, recovery, and regular testing/evaluation), while the contract only requires the processor to provide documentation about its security measures on written request.
results.condition_check: Compatible only in the narrow sense that both relate to security measures and a request trigger, but the contract is about disclosure of documentation rather than the measures themselves.
results.modality_check: The contract uses a mandatory 'shall' for the documentation duty, but that duty is different from the regulatory security-measures obligation.
results.other_constraints_check: The contract adds a written-request condition and a documentation-only target; it does not impose the substantive security, recovery, or testing requirements, nor equivalent or stricter measures.
results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall provide documentation for the Data Processor’s security measures if requested by the Data Controller in writing.

shared_groups: Confidentiality And Personnel Access Controls
results.core_alignment_check: The regulation requires the processor to ensure confidentiality commitments/obligations for persons authorized to process personal data. results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall treat all the Personal Data as strictly confidential information. The Personal Data may not be copied, transferred or otherwise processed in conflict with the Instruction, unless the Data Controller in writing has agreed hereto.

The Data Processor shall on an ongoing basis monitor and control its Sub- Processors’ compliance with the Applicable Law. Documentation of such monitoring and control shall be provided to the Data Controller if so requested in writing.

shared_groups: Liability For Processing Damage

It does not state the processor's liability for damage caused by processing under the GDPR rule.

results.contract_source_text: The Data Processor shall continue to process the Personal Data for up to three months after the termination of the Data Processor Agreement to the extent it is necessary and required under the Applicable Law. In the same period, the Data Processor is entitled to include the Personal Data in the Data Processor’s backup. The Data Processor’s processing of the Data Controller’s Personal Data in the three months after the termination of this Data Processor Agreement shall be considered as being in accordance with the Instruction.
